2605.31410.title: FAM-Bench: A Multimodal Benchmark for Condition-Aware Food-as-Medicine Reasoning
2605.31410.paper_summary: Food-as-Medicine requires models to reason beyond what a dish is or what nutrition it contains: they must decide whether a concrete food choice is appropriate for a specific health condition. Existing food AI benchmarks primarily evaluate dish recognition, recipe understanding, nutrient estimation, or general nutrition question answering, leaving this health-aware decision layer largely untested. We introduce FAM-Bench, a multi-modal Food-as-Medicine benchmark with 2500 nutrition-expert-verified instances across 13 diet-related health conditions. The benchmark contains two complementary tasks: dish-level suitability assessment, where models judge whether a dish is suitable for a condition from its image and ingredient list, and comparative dish analysis, where models rank four candidate dishes by condition-specific suitability. Both tasks require integrating ingredient evidence, visual preparation cues, and clinical nutrition constraints, providing a standardized testbed for grounded health-aware reasoning in language and vision-language models.
2605.31410.paper_summary_zh: Food-as-Medicine 需要模型超越菜餚本身或其所含營養的推理：它們必須決定具體的食物選擇是否適合特定的健康狀況。現有的食品 AI 基準主要評估菜餚識別、食譜理解、營養估算或一般營養問題回答，這使得這個健康意識的決策層面在很大程度上未經測試。我們推出了 FAM-Bench，一個多模態的 Food-as-Medicine 基準，涵蓋 2500 個經營養專家驗證的實例，涉及 13 種與飲食相關的健康狀況。該基準包含兩個互補的任務：菜餚級適宜性評估，模型從菜餚的圖像和成分列表中判斷該菜餚是否適合某種狀況，以及比較菜餚分析，模型根據特定狀況的適宜性對四個候選菜餚進行排名。這兩個任務都需要整合成分證據、視覺準備線索和臨床營養約束，為語言和視覺-語言模型提供了一個標準化的基準，以進行基於健康的推理。
2605.31410.author: Mingyang Mao et.al.
2605.31410.authors: Mingyang Mao, Bhargav Rishi Medisetti, Utkarsh Grover, Tanvir Ibrahim, Wenyan Li, Tingting Zhang, Xiaomin Lin
